text: Cooling System – Overheating Diagnosis If a vehicle overheats after running for 15–30 minutes, the most common causes include: 1. Low coolant level due to leakage 2. Thermostat stuck closed 3. Radiator blockage or restricted airflow 4. Cooling fan failure 5. Water pump malfunction Inspection Steps: - Check coolant reservoir level - Inspect radiator for debris or clogging - Verify cooling fan operation when engine heats - Test thermostat opening temperature - Check for coolant leaks around hoses
emperature - Check for coolant leaks around hoses and joints Symptoms: - Temperature gauge rising rapidly - Steam from engine bay - Coolant smell - Engine knocking at high temperature
